explanation: D correct: CEC Rule 28-200 requires motor branch circuit protection at either 125% FLA or sized for largest motor plus other loads. A and C are both acceptable methods depending on configuration.
B correct: Dry-type transformer sound level is rated in decibels (dB) per NEMA ST 20 standards. A (watts), C (frequency), D (ambient) don't measure sound level.

question_text: IEC motor frame size based on?
explanation: A correct: IEC motor frame sizes are based on shaft height (center of shaft to mounting base) in millimeters. B (HP), C (voltage), D (speed) don't determine frame size designation.

C correct: Anti-plugging relays prevent motor reversal during forward rotation by blocking reverse contactor until motor stops. A (overload), B (overspeed), D (undervoltage) are different protection types.
B correct: Jogging circuits use interlocking to prevent simultaneous energization of jog and run contactors. A (sequence), C (timing), D (none) don't provide the safety interlock needed.
C correct: 3-phase generator synchronizing requires checking both voltage match and phase sequence (rotation). A and B are individual checks; both must be verified before paralleling.
A correct: Closed-transition wye-delta starters maintain power during transition using resistors to limit inrush. B (open), C (no inrush), D (same torque) incorrectly describe closed transition.
A correct: Plugging stops motor by reversing phase sequence while motor runs forward, creating braking torque. B (coasting), C (dynamic braking), D (regenerative) use different stopping methods.
D correct: 3-phase motor vibration comes from all sources - misalignment, voltage/current unbalance, and bent shaft. A, B, C are individual causes; troubleshooting requires checking all.
B correct: Motor current signature analysis (MCSA) detects bearing faults, broken rotor bars, and air gap issues. A (efficiency only), C (temperature only), D (speed only) are too limited.

A correct: Power factor correction capacitors should be located at the load to reduce line losses throughout the system. B (service), C (utility), D (transformer) correct PF but don't reduce facility losses.
D correct: Reduced voltage starting at 85% tap results in all effects - 72% torque (0.85ｲ), 85% current, and slower acceleration. A, B, C are individual effects; all occur together.
B correct: Conversion factor is 1 inch-pound = 0.113 Newton-meters. A, C, D are incorrect conversion factors.

question_text: What happens to harmonics in a 3-phase wye neutral?
explanation: C correct: In 3-phase wye systems, 3rd harmonics (triplen) circulate in the neutral and cancel in the line. A and B are partial effects; both occur together.
C correct: CEC Rule 28-010 for multi-motor installations requires protection based on largest motor plus sum of other motors. A and B are individual requirements; both apply to sizing.
D correct: Transformer paralleling requires all: same voltage ratio, phase sequence, and matched impedance to prevent circulating currents. A, B, C individually; all must match.
A correct: NEMA 3R enclosures are rain-tight for outdoor use, protecting against rain, sleet, and external ice formation. B (indoor) is NEMA 1; C (dust-tight) is NEMA 12; D (corrosion) is NEMA 4X.

C correct: A 3-phase CT secondary must never be opened or left open-circuit while primary carries current - creates dangerous high voltage. A (shorted) is safe for testing; B (grounded) is required; opening creates lethal voltage.

question_text: At what condition is a transformer kVA rating specified?
explanation: A correct: Transformer kVA rating is specified at maximum load (nameplate rating) under standard conditions. B (unity PF), C (80% load), D (no-load) are operating conditions, not rating conditions.
C correct: Wye-delta open transition starters have both power interruption during transition and reduced starting torque. A and B are individual characteristics; both occur in open transition.

A correct: NEMA 1 enclosures are for indoor general-purpose use, protecting against falling dirt and dripping water. B (outdoor), C (corrosive), D (hazardous) require NEMA 3R, 4X, or 7/9 ratings respectively.

question_text: What is a Class CC fuse used for in control circuits?
explanation: C correct: Class CC fuses are both 10kA interrupt rating and fast-acting, making them suitable for control circuits. A and B are individual characteristics; both are required for control circuit protection.

C correct: If overload relay trips on start, likely causes are motor overload, incorrect relay sizing, or relay setting too low for starting current. A (normal), B (ignore), D (bypass) don't address the problem.

D correct: Motor acceleration time too long is caused by all factors - excessive load, inadequate motor torque, low voltage, or high inertia. A, B, C are individual causes; all should be investigated.
A correct: 3-phase megger tests insulation resistance between phases and to ground using high DC voltage (typically 500-1000V). B (continuity), C (voltage), D (phase seq) use different instruments.

question_text: What does a motor nameplate SF 1.0 mean?
explanation: B correct: Motor nameplate SF 1.0 means base rating with no overload capacity allowed. A (no overload) is correct but B is more precise; C (temp rise) and D (voltage) are different parameters.

C correct: Transformer cooling types include both ONAN (Oil Natural Air Natural - passive cooling) and ONAF (Oil Natural Air Forced - with fans). A and B are individual types; both are common cooling methods.
B correct: Transformer DC hi-pot testing detects insulation weakness by applying high DC voltage. A (energizing), C (turns ratio), D (impedance) test different parameters.
A correct: CEC Rule 26-714 specifies raceway sizing for motor circuits based on conductor ampacity and fill percentages. B, C, D cite incorrect rules or requirements.

question_text: What do rotary phase converters produce?
explanation: A correct: Rotary phase converters produce a derived third leg from an idler motor to create 3-phase from single-phase. B (balanced 3f) is ideal but not typical; C (single-phase) is input; D (DC) is incorrect.

question_text: What is the formula for single-phase kVA?
explanation: A correct: Single-phase kVA = V ﾗ I / 1000 converts volt-amperes to kilovolt-amperes. B (v3 V I) is 3-phase; C (Vｲ/R) is power; D (IｲR) is power loss.
A correct: Transformer tap changers adjust voltage by changing turns ratio through tap selection. B (current), C (PF), D (frequency) are not adjusted by taps.

question_text: What does an open delta transformer bank serve?
explanation: A correct: Open delta transformer bank serves 3-phase light loads at 86.6% of full capacity (v3/2). B (full power) requires three transformers; C (motors) need full capacity; D (rectifiers) have different requirements.

question_text: What does a motor service factor of 1.15 allow?
explanation: D correct: Motor service factor 1.15 allows all three - 15% overload capacity, continuous operation at 115%, and higher temperature rise. A, B, C are individual allowances; all are permitted.
A correct: Generator negative sequence relays (46) detect unbalanced loads that cause rotor heating. B (ground fault) is 51G; C (reverse power) is 32; D (overcurrent) is 50/51.
A correct: CEC Rule 28-604 requires overload protection setting at 115% FLA for motors with service factor 1.15 or higher. B (125%), C (150%), D (varies) are for different applications.
A correct: Buck transformers connect primary at 100% tap and use autotransformer action to reduce output voltage. B (boost), C (isolation), D (step-up) describe different transformer types.

question_text: What is a magnetic amplifier in motor controls?
explanation: D correct: Magnetic amplifiers use saturable reactors to control power through DC bias changing AC impedance. A (DC bias), C (saturable reactor) are components; D encompasses the complete operating principle.

A correct: Part-winding starters are for motors with two parallel windings that can be energized sequentially to reduce starting current. B (high inertia), C (frequent start), D (reversing) require different starter types.

B correct: Static phase converters have the limitation of producing unbalanced 3-phase output with reduced power on derived leg. A (expensive), C (large), D (inefficient) are not the primary limitation.

A correct: Thermal protectors inside motors sense winding temperature and open at preset temperature to prevent damage. B (overload relay) is external; C (thermistor) is sensing only; D (all) is incorrect.

question_text: What does a star-delta starter reduce?
explanation: A correct: Star-delta starters reduce starting current to 33% (1/v3) of full voltage starting. B (torque) is also reduced but not the primary benefit; C (both) is partially correct but A is most accurate; D (voltage) is the method, not the benefit.

question_text: What does a solid state soft start replace?
explanation: C correct: CT secondary must never be opened while primary carries current - creates dangerous high voltage. A (grounded) is required; B (loaded) is normal; D (short-circuit) is safer than open.
B correct: Motor cooling fans must rotate in the direction marked on the fan to provide proper airflow over windings. A (either), C (backwards), D (doesn't matter) compromise cooling.

A correct: On delta ungrounded systems, first ground fault detection requires ground detection equipment as circuit remains energized. B (trips immediately) is incorrect - no ground path exists; C, D don't detect first fault.

question_text: What is an across-the-line starter suitable for?
explanation: A correct: Across-the-line starters are suitable for motors under 10HP with low starting torque. B (inverter duty) requires VFD; C (high inertia) needs reduced voltage starting; D (all) is too broad.
B correct: CEC Rule 12-3000 requires transformer rooms to have fire-rated construction with specific ventilation and access requirements. A, C, D are incomplete or incorrect requirements.

question_text: What are the functions of overcurrent relays 50 and 51?
explanation: C correct: Overcurrent relays 50 (instantaneous) and 51 (time-delay) provide both fast and coordinated protection. A and B are individual functions; both work together.

question_text: What does CEC Rule 28-014 specify for group motors?
explanation: D correct: CEC Rule 28-014 allows group motors to have either individual OCP or shared protection based on largest HP. A and C are options; both approaches are code-compliant.
A correct: 3-phase voltage drop formula = v3 ﾗ I ﾗ R ﾗ cosf for balanced loads. B (2 I R) is DC; C (I Z) is impedance drop; D approximates but A is exact.
A correct: Transformer bushing CTs are located in the high voltage bushings for metering and protection. B (low voltage), C (neutral), D (tank) are not standard CT locations.

question_text: What does CEC Rule 8-108 specify for high leg delta marking?
explanation: A correct: CEC Rule 8-108 requires high leg delta (208V phase) to be marked orange for identification. B (red), C (phase A), D (120/208V) are not the high leg marking requirement.
A correct: VFD minimum switching frequency is typically 4-16 kHz to avoid audible motor noise. B, C, D are too low and cause excessive motor hum.
C correct: Corner-grounded delta systems have no neutral conductor - one phase is grounded directly. A (exists), B (floating), D (derives) are incorrect for corner-grounded systems.

D correct: Oil-filled transformer testing includes all methods - Doble (dissolved gas analysis), megger (insulation), and turns ratio tests. A, B, C are individual tests; all are used for comprehensive testing.
B correct: 3-phase motor torque calculation T = (HP ﾗ 5252) / RPM or T = (kW ﾗ 9550) / RPM. A, C, D use incorrect formulas or units.

question_text: What is the purpose of a 3-phase harmonic filter?
explanation: C correct: 3-phase harmonic filters reduce distortion and improve power factor by filtering non-linear load harmonics. A and B are individual benefits; both occur together.
